channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: World Cup: No ICE deployment; extra security for Iran games in Los Angeles
item.description: Federal agents will be present during the World Cup, but immigration enforcement will not occur, county sheriff says.

item.title: Massive fire engulfs Jakarta market leaving hundreds homeless
item.description: Hundreds of people have been made homeless after a huge fire tore through a market in Jakarta.

item.title: Australia, don’t conflate anti-Semitism with criticism of Israel
item.description: The anti-Semitism commission should protect Jewish Australians from hatred, not shield Israel from accountability.

item.title: How does Iran’s leadership view the emerging deal with the US?
item.description: Iranian authorities reject capitulation, but there are subtle differences between various factions.

item.title: Somalia needs a political settlement before it is too late
item.description: Somalia is entering a dangerous moment without a path to elections or a political transition.

item.title: High Court orders Kenya government to release details of Ebola facility
item.description: Hundreds of people took to the streets in the central Kenyan town of Nanyuki to protest against the planned site.

item.title: ‘Like mice in a cage’: Inside Europe’s prison overcrowding crisis
item.description: In some of Europe’s richest countries, including Belgium, overcrowding is driving a severe deterioration of conditions.

item.title: Tennis stars rejoice as Serena Williams announces competitive comeback
item.description: The 44-year-old will return at the Queen's Club Championships this month after receiving women's doubles wildcard entry.

item.title: France World Cup 2026 preview: Players to watch, group matches and squad
item.description: Departing Deschamps looks to win second title with side led by superstar forwards Kylian Mbappe and Ousmane Dembele.

item.title: Huge Messi statue taken down in India over safety fears
item.description: Huge Messi statue taken down in India over safety fears.

item.title: Nigerian drifters build track, dream of Formula One
item.description: Nigerian drifters build track, dream of Formula One

item.title: Norway salvages 18th-century shipwreck, recovers porcelain and chandelier
item.description: Archaeologists recovered Chinese porcelain and European goods from an 18th-century shipwreck found in southern Norway.

item.title: Hailstorm hits Denver and causes widespread disruption
item.description: A powerful hailstorm swept through the US city of Denver, bringing hailstones reportedly as large as golf balls.

item.title: Mozambique says 5 citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
item.description: The violence prompted 300 Mozambicans to return home over the weekend.

item.title: China opens its markets to African exports. Who benefits?
item.description: China's tariff-free access for many African products has renewed hopes of industrial growth. But trade remains uneven.

item.title: Senegal president names government, boycotted by ally-turned-rival
item.description: Ousted PM says his party, which holds the parliamentary majority, will not participate in the new government.

item.title: At least 12 people killed, dozens wounded in Russian attacks on Ukraine
item.description: Russia launched 656 drones and 73 missiles at Ukraine overnight, according to Ukraine's air force.

item.title: South Africa’s World Cup delegation departs for Mexico without coach
item.description: Bafana Bafana's departure was delayed due to non-issuance of visas for several players and support staff.

item.title: Iran war day 95: Trump pushes Lebanon truce after Tehran vows to end talks
item.description: Trump says he persuaded Israel and Hezbollah to halt attacks, seeking to prevent a wider war in Lebanon.

item.title: India’s Zee Entertainment signs World Cup 2026 broadcast deal with FIFA
item.description: Zee will broadcast the 2026 and 2030 World Cups and the 2027 Women's World Cup among 39 FIFA tournaments until 2034.

item.title: Rafael Grossi: the next Iran nuclear deal will look very different
item.description: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi says the 2015 Iran nuclear deal is no longer a workable model.

item.title: Mexican protesters clash with police ahead of the 2026 World Cup
item.description: Teachers marched in Mexico demanding better pay and pensions, warning of further protests ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Google parent Alphabet to sell $80bn in stock to fund AI plans
item.description: US tech giant says fundraising drive includes deal to sell $10bn of stock to Berkshire Hathaway.

item.title: Woman assaulted by Dutch police at asylum centre speaks to Al Jazeera
item.description: Malak Mahmoud, the pregnant woman filmed being thrown to the ground by a Dutch police officer has spoken to Al Jazeera.

item.title: US Defense Department bars journalists from its press office
item.description: Media freedom advocates condemn move as latest effort to curtail independent reporting on the US military.
